CREATE TABLE categories (id INTEGER, category VARCHAR);
SELECT category, list_aggregate(num, 'min') FROM numbers JOIN categories ON id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY category;
SELECT category, list_aggregate(num, 'count') FROM numbers JOIN categories ON id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY category;
CREATE TABLE temp (id INTEGER, nums INTEGER[]);
INSERT INTO temp VALUES (1, ARRAY[1, 2, 3]), (2, ARRAY[NULL, NULL, 4]);
SELECT id, list_aggregate(nums, 'min') FROM temp GROUP BY id;
SELECT id, list_aggregate(nums, 'max') FROM temp WHERE id = 1 GROUP BY id;
WITH aggregated AS (SELECT list_aggregate(num, 'count') AS count_num FROM numbers) SELECT * FROM aggregated WHERE count_num > 0;
CREATE TABLE test_data (group_id INTEGER, values INTEGER[]);
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (1, ARRAY[2, 3, NULL]), (1, ARRAY[4, 5]), (2, ARRAY[NULL, 1]);
SELECT group_id, list_aggregate(values, 'min') FROM test_data GROUP BY group_id;
SELECT group_id, list_aggregate(values, 'max') FROM test_data WHERE group_id = 1;
SELECT group_id, list_aggregate(values, 'count') AS total_count FROM test_data GROUP BY group_id HAVING total_count > 1;
